args.title: Same-hour safeguarding review for Leo

args.body: Thank you for reaching out about your child. A member of our clinical team will follow up with you directly. We're here to help and will be in touch shortly.

items.recommended_next_action: Clinical lead to review the escalation this hour and determine mandated-reporter obligations before any routine intake or scheduling.
items.draft_reply: Thank you for reaching out about your child. A member of our clinical team will follow up with you directly. We're here to help and will be in touch shortly.

escalation.reason: Possible child-safety disclosure; safeguarding policy requires immediate clinical escalation.

items.decision_rationale: Safeguarding signal detected (possible harm or unsafe caregiving). Per policy this is forced to P0 with immediate clinical escalation, regardless of the requested service. Model note: SAFEGUARDING SIGNAL PRESENT: Mother reports child 'more clingy since his dad started getting rough with him during weekends.' This language indicates potential physical abuse, rough handling, or aggressive parenting behavior that may constitute child maltreatment. Regardless of whether 'rough' is intended as colloquial or literal, the stated correlation between paternal roughness and behavioral change (increased clinginess) is a mandated-reporter concern. This requires same-hour human (clinical leadership/designated reporter) review before any scheduling action. The speech therapy request is secondary to the safety concern. No P1 scheduling accommodation should occur until safeguarding assessment is completed.

args.notes: Parent asked a developmental question. Per policy, do not answer clinically by message; offer a screening/evaluation pathway.
args.title: Route clinical question to screening for Ava

args.body: Thank you for your question. Every child develops at their own pace, and the best way to get a clear answer is a short screening with one of our speech-language pathologists. We can't give clinical advice by message, but we'd be glad to set up an evaluation so a clinician can take a look.

items.recommended_next_action: Intake to offer a screening/evaluation appointment; a clinician (not the front desk) addresses the developmental question.
items.draft_reply: Thank you for your question. Every child develops at their own pace, and the best way to get a clear answer is a short screening with one of our speech-language pathologists. We can't give clinical advice by message, but we'd be glad to set up an evaluation so a clinician can take a look.
items.decision_rationale: Parent is asking a normative/developmental question about R sound acquisition and seeking clinical guidance before committing to a referral. This is a common developmental question (rhotacism is typical in 4-year-olds and often resolves by age 6–7). Classification is clinical_question rather than new_referral because the parent explicitly states they want advice before booking. Urgency is P2 (standard intake workflow) — no safety concern, no same-day operational need, and the question is developmental/educational in nature. The clinic should prepare a response addressing typical R-sound development, red flags for concern, and conditions under which evaluation would be recommended, then invite the family to schedule if appropriate.

escalation.reason: Same-day reschedule requires prompt front-desk action today.

items.decision_rationale: Existing patient (Noah Patel, DOB known, OT discipline confirmed) with a same-day cancellation request for a 3pm appointment. Multiple exclamation marks and 'URGENT' indicate parent stress, but the substance is a straightforward reschedule need. This is P1 because staff must act today to cancel/reschedule the 3pm slot. No safeguarding concern (vomiting at school is presented as reason for absence, not as harm). No clinical advice being requested.
